reason: Answer A (with-skill) focuses exclusively on Sam-relevant context: his role, the Q3 roadmap alignment, the auth latency demo requirement, and the billing deferral decision - then closes with concrete decision items. Answer B (baseline) includes the same Sam/Dana memories but pads the output with unrelated auth hotfix, token-refresh endpoint, load test, and eventstore content that has no bearing on a 1:1 with Sam. The extra noise dilutes the briefing and forces the user to filter. Both answers are grounded, but A is more relevant and useful by a clear margin.
